calendar.matters:
Petition To Confirm Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jul-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Hespenheide appeared by zoom for Meriel Lindley and Paul Grippardi.

Ms. Hespenheide stated that the dividends are from four different family trusts, but for one quarter and that they are same dividends/all of the dividends declared by Hormel foods on May 24th. Ms. Hespenheide also spoke of the difference between the will and the most recent restatement/11th restatement, stated that the 11th restatement was executed only several days before Mr. Hormel's passing, that he passed away very unexpectedly while in the hospital, and that there was no schedule A attached to the 11th restatement, but they have the schedule A from the prior restatement, and it's laid out in the general assignment. In addition, Ms. Hespenheide stated that she believes this shows that the decedent's intent was that all of the benefits that he was consistently receiving during his lifetime every quarter from the family trusts be part of his trust estate, and that this was his pattern and practice, and there were wire transfers that were set up. Furthermore, Ms. Hespenheide stated that she has a good relationship the financial institution and Hormel, that they sent a letter saying that there is no question that the decedent is entitled to these dividends, that they are both willing to go with the Heggstad procedure, and that they were just trying to save time and money, but they need a court order. The court referred to paragraph three of the proposed order and stated that it would not put a time limit on it and that if the order is not complied with, there could be other proceedings. The court finds that no notice is needed to Michael's heirs. The Petition to Confirm Trust Assets is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: H. Singh. Not reported.

Petition To Confirm Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Continued To Jun-29-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
